questions.question: Write recursive functions for inorder, preorder, and postorder traversals of a binary tree.
keyPoints: Inorder: recurse left, visit node, recurse right (L-N-R)
Preorder: visit node, recurse left, recurse right (N-L-R)
Postorder: recurse left, recurse right, visit node (L-R-N)
Base case: if node is NULL, return
Time: O(n), Space: O(h) for recursion stack

questions.question: Write a function for level order traversal of a binary tree using a queue.
keyPoints: Create a queue and enqueue root
While queue is not empty: dequeue, print, enqueue children
Enqueue left child first, then right child
Time: O(n), Space: O(w) where w is maximum width

questions.question: Given the preorder and inorder traversal sequences of a binary tree, construct the binary tree and find the postorder traversal.
keyPoints: First element of preorder is the root
Find root in inorder to split left and right subtrees
Recursively construct left and right subtrees
Derive postorder from the constructed tree
Example with step-by-step construction

questions.question: Write a non-recursive (iterative) inorder traversal algorithm using an explicit stack.
keyPoints: Use an explicit stack to simulate recursion
Push all left children onto the stack
Pop and process node, then move to right child
Repeat until stack is empty and current is NULL
Time: O(n), Space: O(h)

questions.question: Compare depth-first traversals (inorder, preorder, postorder) with breadth-first traversal (level order). Discuss their applications.
keyPoints: DFS uses stack (implicit or explicit), BFS uses queue
DFS space: O(h), BFS space: O(w)
Inorder: sorted output from BST
Preorder: serialize/copy tree
Postorder: delete tree, evaluate expression tree
Level order: find shortest path, print level by level
